response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web software developer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and some low-latency DLang systems work, but the role requires deep hardware-oriented expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. While the BS plus 15+ years of experience satisfies the formal education/experience requirement, the domain mismatch is fundamental for a principal-level performance architect position.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career (15+ years) spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Some low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang async I/O services handling millions of requests/sec)
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship) and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance-modeling experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or accelerator optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI accelerator / GPU / DNN workload background
Solid general software engineering with some performance-sensitive systems work
